methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Prusa Research is a bootstrapped manufacturer and robotics company founded by Josef Průša, operating in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector. The comparable universe consists of nine firms: one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The sector contains seven same-sector robotics peers, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in each cohort. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and capital requirements across early-stage, growth, and late-stage portfolio companies. No triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from a single public comparable or unfiltered private peer set without introducing arbitrary weighting.

A credible valuation requires either: (1) access to recent transaction data or secondary market pricing for Prusa or direct peers; (2) detailed financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, growth rate) enabling revenue or earnings multiple analysis; or (3) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent hardware-manufacturing or vertical automation businesses with tighter financial transparency. The bootstrapped status and manufacturing-hardware positioning further complicate benchmarking against venture-backed software or service robotics platforms. No defensible valuation estimate is supportable from the available data.
